intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, encouraging tone over clinical language when logging health data
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Warm check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward personal, supportive messaging; dashboard should feel like a coach or friend, not a medical system
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers neutral, straightforward language; dashboard should be efficient and emotionally restrained

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, eased transitions when viewing new health data over instant content swaps
facade.axisTargeted: Motion continuity vs instant state change
facade.label: Smooth slide-in
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard favors animated micro-interactions and gentle transitions between screens/sections
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should use instant, no-motion state changes—prioritize speed and directness over fluidity

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, encouraging tone over clinical language when logging health data
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward personal, supportive messaging; dashboard should feel like a coach or friend, not a medical system

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent left navigation paired with centered, card-based content areas for health experiment tracking
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure and content layout organization
facade.label: Sidebar with floating cards
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward persistent context (sidebar) + modular, scannable content (cards) for experiment dashboards
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tab-based navigation, list-based layouts, or full-width single-column structures instead

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard with warm, diffused lighting and gentle shadows will feel more approachable and less clinical than bright, cold interfaces
facade.label: Soft warm light
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers interfaces that feel nurturing and human-centered over sterile/minimal; likely to accept rounded shapes, warm colors, and organic layouts
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clarity-first design with high contrast and cool tones; data readability and visual hierarchy take priority over ambient warmth

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer calming, desaturated palettes that reduce visual noise and emphasize data clarity over aesthetic stimulation
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + calm vs energetic
facade.label: Soft monochrome
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use muted grays, soft blacks, and subtle tonal hierarchies; minimize color as decoration; let data breathe
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer either chromatic richness (warm tones, accent colors) or higher contrast/crispness (bright whites, bold blacks); not drawn to restraint-focused design

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users optimizing health experiments want immediate metric comprehension (large, prominent values) over trend visualization, because they're tracking multiple variables and need at-a-glance status before diving into detail
facade.axisTargeted: Information hierarchy — do prominent data points (KPIs) lead, or do trend visualizations and patterns?
facade.label: Big numbers, small charts
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard leads with large metric cards (weight: 187 lbs, sleep: 7.2 hrs, HRV: 52) with tiny sparklines underneath. Text-first, visualization-supporting.
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard leads with medium-sized charts and graphs, with metric values embedded as labels or secondary text. Visualization-first hierarchy.

facade.hypothesis: Users want positive reinforcement and milestone celebrations in health tracking, not just neutral data logging—creating emotional investment in their progress
facade.label: Celebratory notifications
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should trigger visual/copy celebrations for streaks, goal completions, or personal bests; users feel motivated by wins, not just informed by data
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer understated, matter-of-fact feedback that doesn't dramatize health progress; celebration may feel patronizing or undermine serious health intent

draft.title: Health Experiment Tracker
draft.summary: A personal dashboard for monitoring long-term health experiments with experiment cards, metrics, and progress visualization.
draft.html:
Health Experiments

Track your long-term health initiatives

Active Experiments

Sleep Optimization

Active

Testing consistent 10 PM bedtime with blue light reduction

Week 4 of 8 65% complete
Avg Sleep Duration 7.2 hrs
Sleep Quality 8.1/10
Consistency 92%

Hydration Protocol

Active

Drinking 3L water daily and tracking energy levels

Week 2 of 12 42% complete
Daily Intake 2.8L
Energy Level 7.4/10
Days Logged 14/14
Completed Experiments

Morning Meditation

Completed

30-day meditation practice (completed)

Final Result Positive
